syntax_3372.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3372.title: Player Tabs Are Visible
syntax_3372.description: Checks whether the specified or all tablist(s) allow player tabs to be visible. Setting this to false prevents any player tabs from being seen in the specified or all tablist(s) (players who join will be automatically hidden). Setting this to true immediately makes all player tabs visible in the specified or all tablist(s). Use the Show or Hide in Tablist effect if you would like to set this condition to be true without immediately showing all players. Note that it is possible for this condition/expression to be true yet no player tabs are visible if they are hidden manually using the Show or Hide in Tablist effect. In this case, players who join will still be visible in the tablist unless manually hidden using the effect.
syntax_3372.syntax_pattern:
player tabs (are|aren't|are not) visible [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
[(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist[s] (contains|(does|do|doesn't|does not|don't|do not) contain) players
[the] tablist[s] [of (%-players%|group %-string%)] (contains|(does|do|doesn't|does not|don't|do not) contain) players
players are (visible|hidden) in [the] tablist[s] [(for|of) (%-players%|group %-string%)]
players are (visible|hidden) in [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist[s]

en: Checks whether the specified or all tablist(s) allow player tabs to be visible. Setting this to false prevents any player tabs from being seen in the specified or all tablist(s) (players who join will be automatically hidden). Setting this to true immediately makes all player tabs visible in the specified or all tablist(s). Use the Show or Hide in Tablist effect if you would like to set this condition to be true without immediately showing all players. Note that it is possible for this condition/expression to be true yet no player tabs are visible if they are hidden manually using the Show or Hide in Tablist effect. In this case, players who join will still be visible in the tablist unless manually hidden using the effect.

syntax_3376.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3376.title: Enable or Disable Array Tablist
syntax_3376.description: Enables or disables array tablist in the specified or all tablist(s) The array tablist creates a grid of tabs that allows you to use grid coordinates to easily modify individual tabs. When enabling, you can specify the amount of columns (defaults to 4), the amount of rows (defaults to 20), and the initial icon (defaults to a white texture). See the Size of Array Tablist and Icon of Array Tab expressions for more info. Note that it is recommended to use the default tablist dimensions of 4x20 to avoid issues with skins. If the array tablist is already enabled, it will be replaced with a blank one matching the new specifications. Note that when enabling the array tablist, all simple tabs will be removed and all player tabs will be hidden. When disabling the array tablist, all player tabs will be visible, and there will be no simple tabs.
syntax_3376.syntax_pattern:
(enable|activate) [the] array tablist [for (%-players%|group %-string%)] [with [%-number% columns] [%-number% rows] [(default|initial) (head|icon|player_head) %-skin%]]
(disable|deactivate) [the] array tablist [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]

en: Enables or disables array tablist in the specified or all tablist(s) The array tablist creates a grid of tabs that allows you to use grid coordinates to easily modify individual tabs. When enabling, you can specify the amount of columns (defaults to 4), the amount of rows (defaults to 20), and the initial icon (defaults to a white texture). See the Size of Array Tablist and Icon of Array Tab expressions for more info. Note that it is recommended to use the default tablist dimensions of 4x20 to avoid issues with skins. If the array tablist is already enabled, it will be replaced with a blank one matching the new specifications. Note that when enabling the array tablist, all simple tabs will be removed and all player tabs will be hidden. When disabling the array tablist, all player tabs will be visible, and there will be no simple tabs.

syntax_3848.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3848.title: Color Blueprint
syntax_3848.description: Colors all of the specified blueprint as the specified color. Normally the blueprint you specify will be a section of a larger blueprint, as we normally don't need skins that are a single color.

en: Colors all of the specified blueprint as the specified color. Normally the blueprint you specify will be a section of a larger blueprint, as we normally don't need skins that are a single color.

syntax_3849.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3849.title: Create Blueprint
syntax_3849.description: Creates a blueprint and puts it in the specified variable (the %object% at the end). This is a delayed effect as it requires retrieving image data either from online or from a file, so if used inside of a function the function will return before the effect (and any code after the effect) completes. The first syntax creates the blueprint from an existing skin. The second syntax creates from an image at the specified url or file path. If the blueprint is meant to be a blueprint of a certain part of a skin (ex. the head, right leg, etc.), a part in the second layer of a skin, or even a certain face (front, back, top, etc.) of a part, you can specify that. You can also specify whether it is meant to be a slim skin (skin with thinner arms).
syntax_3849.syntax_pattern:
create blueprint of %skin% in %object%
create [(slim)] [(head|body|right arm|left arm|right leg|left leg) [(front|back|right|left|top|bottom) face]] blueprint from (file|url) %string% in %object%

en: Creates a blueprint and puts it in the specified variable (the %object% at the end). This is a delayed effect as it requires retrieving image data either from online or from a file, so if used inside of a function the function will return before the effect (and any code after the effect) completes. The first syntax creates the blueprint from an existing skin. The second syntax creates from an image at the specified url or file path. If the blueprint is meant to be a blueprint of a certain part of a skin (ex. the head, right leg, etc.), a part in the second layer of a skin, or even a certain face (front, back, top, etc.) of a part, you can specify that. You can also specify whether it is meant to be a slim skin (skin with thinner arms).

syntax_3850.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3850.title: Overlay Blueprint on Blueprint
syntax_3850.description: Overlays the first specified blueprint on the second specified blueprint. To understand what "overlay" means, you can imagine the blueprints being pieces of paper (possibly with transparency), and just putting one piece of paper on top of the other. So if the first specified blueprint is fully opaque, then it will completely replace the second specified blueprint, which is sometimes what you need. Note that the overlaying is done by matching the top left corner of both blueprints, so if the first specified blueprint is smaller than the second, then the lower and or the right pixels of the second specified blueprint won't have anything overlayed on them.

en: Overlays the first specified blueprint on the second specified blueprint. To understand what "overlay" means, you can imagine the blueprints being pieces of paper (possibly with transparency), and just putting one piece of paper on top of the other. So if the first specified blueprint is fully opaque, then it will completely replace the second specified blueprint, which is sometimes what you need. Note that the overlaying is done by matching the top left corner of both blueprints, so if the first specified blueprint is smaller than the second, then the lower and or the right pixels of the second specified blueprint won't have anything overlayed on them.

syntax_3851.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3851.title: Recolor Blueprint
syntax_3851.description: Recolors all pixels with the first specified color in the specified blueprint as having the second specified color.

en: Recolors all pixels with the first specified color in the specified blueprint as having the second specified color.

syntax_3852.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3852.title: Blank Blueprint
syntax_3852.description: A new, blank blueprint. All pixels are colored transparent by default, which for the first layer means that they will render as black (but still act as transparent when using blueprint syntaxes).If the blueprint is meant to be a blueprint of a certain part of a skin (ex. the head, right leg, etc.), a part in the second layer of a skin, or even a certain face (front, back, top, etc.) of a part, you can specify that. You can also specify whether it is meant to be a slim skin (skin with thinner arms).
syntax_3852.syntax_pattern: blank [(slim)] [ (head|body|right arm|left arm|right leg|left leg) [(front|back|right|left|top|bottom) face]] blueprint

en: A new, blank blueprint. All pixels are colored transparent by default, which for the first layer means that they will render as black (but still act as transparent when using blueprint syntaxes).If the blueprint is meant to be a blueprint of a certain part of a skin (ex. the head, right leg, etc.), a part in the second layer of a skin, or even a certain face (front, back, top, etc.) of a part, you can specify that. You can also specify whether it is meant to be a slim skin (skin with thinner arms).

syntax_3853.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3853.title: Body Part of Blueprint
syntax_3853.description: A blueprint containing the region of the specified blueprint representing the specified body part, optionally restricted to a certain face (front, back, left, right, top, bottom) of the body part. You can specify whether the blueprint should be of the first (main) layer of the body part, or the second layer (which can have transparency).
syntax_3853.syntax_pattern: [(second layer)] (head|body|right arm|left arm|right leg|left leg) [(front|back|right|left|top|bottom) face] pixels of %blueprint%

en: A blueprint containing the region of the specified blueprint representing the specified body part, optionally restricted to a certain face (front, back, left, right, top, bottom) of the body part. You can specify whether the blueprint should be of the first (main) layer of the body part, or the second layer (which can have transparency).

syntax_3854.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3854.title: Copy of Blueprint
syntax_3854.description: Produces a copy of the specified blueprint. Changes made to the copy won't affect the original, so this might be used, for example, if you have a template that you modify to produce skins.

en: Produces a copy of the specified blueprint. Changes made to the copy won't affect the original, so this might be used, for example, if you have a template that you modify to produce skins.

syntax_3855.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3855.title: Color of Pixel in Blueprint
syntax_3855.description: The color of the specified pixel in the specified blueprint.

en: The color of the specified pixel in the specified blueprint.

syntax_3856.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3856.title: Face of Body Part Blueprint
syntax_3856.description: A blueprint containing the region of the specified blueprint representing the specified face of the body part. The specified blueprint must represent a single body part in a skin.

en: A blueprint containing the region of the specified blueprint representing the specified face of the body part. The specified blueprint must represent a single body part in a skin.

syntax_3857.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3857.title: Rectangle of Blueprint
syntax_3857.description: A blueprint consisting of a rectangular region of the specified blueprint, delineated by the two specified corners of the rectangle. Changes made to the returned blueprint will also be made to the corresponding pixels in the original. You can additionally use the Copy of Blueprint expression if you don't want this to happen.

en: A blueprint consisting of a rectangular region of the specified blueprint, delineated by the two specified corners of the rectangle. Changes made to the returned blueprint will also be made to the corresponding pixels in the original. You can additionally use the Copy of Blueprint expression if you don't want this to happen.

syntax_3400.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3400.title: Priority of Simple Tab
syntax_3400.description: This is the priority of the specified simple tab in the specified or all tablist(s). The priority is a string (at most 12 characters) that determines the position that the specified simple tab will take relative to the other simple tabs. Specifically, simple tabs are arranged alphabetically by their priority. By default the priority is the first 12 characters of the simple tab's id.

en: This is the priority of the specified simple tab in the specified or all tablist(s). The priority is a string (at most 12 characters) that determines the position that the specified simple tab will take relative to the other simple tabs. Specifically, simple tabs are arranged alphabetically by their priority. By default the priority is the first 12 characters of the simple tab's id.

syntax_3858.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3858.title: ARGB Color
syntax_3858.description: A color that can have transparency; 'rgb' stands for the usual red, green, blue values (from 0 to 255 inclusive), and 'a' stands for the alpha value that describes transparency, where 0 means fully transparent, and 255 means fully opaque. When using normal Skript colors (green, blue, etc.) as argb colors, they will be converted as fully opaque. You can also write argb colors as hex codes: `rgb RRGGBB` will be a fully opaque argb color from the hex code RRGGBB, and `argb AARRGGBB` will be an argb colors with transparency AA and rgb hex code RRGGBB.

en: A color that can have transparency; 'rgb' stands for the usual red, green, blue values (from 0 to 255 inclusive), and 'a' stands for the alpha value that describes transparency, where 0 means fully transparent, and 255 means fully opaque. When using normal Skript colors (green, blue, etc.) as argb colors, they will be converted as fully opaque. You can also write argb colors as hex codes: `rgb RRGGBB` will be a fully opaque argb color from the hex code RRGGBB, and `argb AARRGGBB` will be an argb colors with transparency AA and rgb hex code RRGGBB.

syntax_3859.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3859.title: Blueprint
syntax_3859.description: A blueprint of a skin that can be modified in Skript and then used to create an actual skin that you can put on players. A blueprint could be for a whole skin or be a section of another blueprint.

en: A blueprint of a skin that can be modified in Skript and then used to create an actual skin that you can put on players. A blueprint could be for a whole skin or be a section of another blueprint.

syntax_3371.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3371.title: Array Tablist is Enabled
syntax_3371.description: Checks whether the array tablist is enabled or disabled in the specified or all tablist(s). See the Enable or Disable Array Tablist effect for more info.

en: Checks whether the array tablist is enabled or disabled in the specified or all tablist(s). See the Enable or Disable Array Tablist effect for more info.

syntax_3373.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3373.title: Player Tab is Visible
syntax_3373.description: Checks whether the first player's tab is visible in the specified or all tablist(s).
syntax_3373.syntax_pattern:
[the] [player] tab of %player% is (visible|hidden) [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
%player%'s [player] tab is (visible|hidden) [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
%player% is (visible|hidden) in [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist[s]
%player% is (visible|hidden) in [the] tablist[s] [(for|of) (%-players%|group %-string%)]

en: Checks whether the first player's tab is visible in the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3374.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3374.title: Scores are Enabled
syntax_3374.description: Checks whether the specified or all tablist(s) have scores enabled. This only applies to enabling scores using MundoSK's tablist syntaxes.
syntax_3374.syntax_pattern:
scores [are] (enabled|disabled) in [the] tablist[s] [of (%-players%|group %-string%)]
scores [are] (enabled|disabled) in [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist[s]

en: Checks whether the specified or all tablist(s) have scores enabled. This only applies to enabling scores using MundoSK's tablist syntaxes.

syntax_3375.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3375.title: Add or Remove Columns or Rows in Tablist
syntax_3375.description: Adds or removes the specified amount of rows or columns to the specified or all tablist(s), optionally giving those rows/columns the specified icon instead of the default for the tablist(s).
syntax_3375.syntax_pattern:
add ([a] (column|row)|%-number% (column|row)s) [with icon %-skin%] to [the] [array] tablist[s] [of (%-players%|group %-string%)]
add ([a] (column|row)|%-number% (column|row)s) [with icon %-skin%] to [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] [array] tablist[s]
remove ([a] (column|row)|%-number% (column|row)s) from [the] [array] tablist[s] [of (%-players%|group %-string%)]
remove ([a] (column|row)|%-number% (column|row)s) from [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] [array] tablist[s]

en: Adds or removes the specified amount of rows or columns to the specified or all tablist(s), optionally giving those rows/columns the specified icon instead of the default for the tablist(s).

syntax_3377.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3377.title: Clear Player Tab Modifications
syntax_3377.description: Resets all of the tabs representing players in the specified or all tablist(s) to normal. This will make all players visible in the tablist(s) and reset any display name, latency, and score changes.

en: Resets all of the tabs representing players in the specified or all tablist(s) to normal. This will make all players visible in the tablist(s) and reset any display name, latency, and score changes.

syntax_3378.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3378.title: Show or Hide in Tablist
syntax_3378.description: Shows or hides certain players in the specified or all tablist(s). Note: if the Players Are Visible condition/expression is set to false in a certain tablist and you show a player in that tablist using this effect, then the condition/expression will become true but only that player will become unhidden.
syntax_3378.syntax_pattern:
(show|hide) [the] [player] tab[s] of %players% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
(show|hide) %players%'[s] [player] tab[s] [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
(show|hide) %players% in [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist[s]
(show|hide) %players% in [the] tablist[s] [of (%-players%|group %-string%)]
(show|hide) %players% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)] in [the] tablist[s]

en: Shows or hides certain players in the specified or all tablist(s). Note: if the Players Are Visible condition/expression is set to false in a certain tablist and you show a player in that tablist using this effect, then the condition/expression will become true but only that player will become unhidden.

syntax_3380.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3380.title: Delete All Simple Tabs
syntax_3380.description: Removes all simple tabs from the specified or all tablist(s).

en: Removes all simple tabs from the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3381.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3381.title: Delete Simple Tab
syntax_3381.description: Removes the simple tab with the specified id from the specified or all tablist(s)

en: Removes the simple tab with the specified id from the specified or all tablist(s)

syntax_3382.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3382.title: Toggle Scores in Tablist
syntax_3382.description: Enables or disables scores in the specified or all tablist(s).This only applies to scores using MundoSK's tablist syntaxes.
syntax_3382.syntax_pattern:
(enable|disable) scores in [the] tablist[s] [of (%-players%|group %-string%)]
(enable|disable) scores in [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist[s]

en: Enables or disables scores in the specified or all tablist(s).This only applies to scores using MundoSK's tablist syntaxes.

syntax_3379.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3379.title: Create Simple Tab
syntax_3379.description: Creates a simple tab in the specified or all tablist(s) with the specified id and properties. If a specified player already has a simple tab with the specified id in their tablist, that tab will be deleted before creating the new tab. This effect will not work for a specified player if they have the array tablist enabled.

en: Creates a simple tab in the specified or all tablist(s) with the specified id and properties. If a specified player already has a simple tab with the specified id in their tablist, that tab will be deleted before creating the new tab. This effect will not work for a specified player if they have the array tablist enabled.

syntax_3397.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3397.title: Display Name of Simple Tab
syntax_3397.description: An expression for the display name of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s).

en: An expression for the display name of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3398.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3398.title: Icon of Simple Tab
syntax_3398.description: An expression for the icon of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s).

en: An expression for the icon of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3399.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3399.title: Latency Bars of Simple Tab
syntax_3399.description: An expression for the amount of latency bars of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s). This is always between 0 and 5.

en: An expression for the amount of latency bars of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s). This is always between 0 and 5.

syntax_3384.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3384.title: Delete Tablist Group
syntax_3384.description: Removes all players from the specified tablist group. Like the Remove Players from Tablist Group effect, this will not make any modifications to the tablists of those players. This will also reset modifications that have been applied to the specified tablist group, meaning that if a new player were to be added to the group, modifications applied to the group before it was deleted would not be applied to the player. If you don't want to reset these modifications, see the Empty Tablist Group effect.

en: Removes all players from the specified tablist group. Like the Remove Players from Tablist Group effect, this will not make any modifications to the tablists of those players. This will also reset modifications that have been applied to the specified tablist group, meaning that if a new player were to be added to the group, modifications applied to the group before it was deleted would not be applied to the player. If you don't want to reset these modifications, see the Empty Tablist Group effect.

syntax_3385.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3385.title: Empty Tablist Group
syntax_3385.description: Removes all players from the specified tablist group. Like the Remove Players from Tablist Group effect, this will not make any modifications to the tablists of those players. This will also not reset modifications that have been applied to the specified tablist group, meaning that if a new player were to be added to the group, modifications applied to the group previously would be applied to the player. If you also want to reset these modifications, see the Delete Tablist Group effect.

en: Removes all players from the specified tablist group. Like the Remove Players from Tablist Group effect, this will not make any modifications to the tablists of those players. This will also not reset modifications that have been applied to the specified tablist group, meaning that if a new player were to be added to the group, modifications applied to the group previously would be applied to the player. If you also want to reset these modifications, see the Delete Tablist Group effect.

syntax_3386.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3386.title: Remove Players from Tablist Group
syntax_3386.description: Removes the specified players from the specified tablist group. This effect will not make any modifications to the tablists of the specified players.

en: Removes the specified players from the specified tablist group. This effect will not make any modifications to the tablists of the specified players.

syntax_3388.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3388.title: Dimension of Array Tablist
syntax_3388.description:
An expression for the amount of rows or columns in the specified or all array tablist(s). There can be 1 to 4 columns.For each amount of columns, there is a specified range for the amount of rows:
1 Column: 1 to 20 rows
2 Columns: 11 to 20 rows
3 Columns: 14 to 20 rows
4 Columns: 16 to 20 rows
This is due to the fact that Minecraft allows 1 to 80 total tabs, and for each amount, there is only one way the tablist can appear. Minecraft only allows a maximum of 20 tabs in one column, so the tabs will try to fill as few columns as possible will adhering to this rule. For example, if there are 40 tabs, this is satisfied by a 2x20 tablist, but for 41 and 42 you need 3x14.

en:
An expression for the amount of rows or columns in the specified or all array tablist(s). There can be 1 to 4 columns.For each amount of columns, there is a specified range for the amount of rows:
1 Column: 1 to 20 rows
2 Columns: 11 to 20 rows
3 Columns: 14 to 20 rows
4 Columns: 16 to 20 rows
This is due to the fact that Minecraft allows 1 to 80 total tabs, and for each amount, there is only one way the tablist can appear. Minecraft only allows a maximum of 20 tabs in one column, so the tabs will try to fill as few columns as possible will adhering to this rule. For example, if there are 40 tabs, this is satisfied by a 2x20 tablist, but for 41 and 42 you need 3x14.

syntax_3389.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3389.title: Icon of Array Tab
syntax_3389.description: An expression for either the icon of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s).

en: An expression for either the icon of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3390.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3390.title: Latency Bars of Array Tab
syntax_3390.description: An expression for the amount of latency bars of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s). This is always an integer between 0 and 5 (inclusive).

en: An expression for the amount of latency bars of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s). This is always an integer between 0 and 5 (inclusive).

syntax_3391.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3391.title: Score of Array Tab
syntax_3391.description: An expression for the score of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s).

en: An expression for the score of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3392.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3392.title: Owner of Player Head
syntax_3392.description: An expression for the owner of the specified player head, as an item or placed. The owner only means the name that is shown when held, like "Tlatoani's Head", and doesn't affect the actual skin that the player head has.

en: An expression for the owner of the specified player head, as an item or placed. The owner only means the name that is shown when held, like "Tlatoani's Head", and doesn't affect the actual skin that the player head has.

syntax_3393.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3393.title: Player Head from Skin
syntax_3393.description: An expression for a player head bearing the specified skin, optionally with the specified owner. If you do not specify an owner, the owner will appear to be "MundoSK-Name". This really only matters if anybody is going to actually have the player head in their inventory (i.e. if the player head is only going to be used as a block, the owner isn't important, though it may be useful as an identifier).

en: An expression for a player head bearing the specified skin, optionally with the specified owner. If you do not specify an owner, the owner will appear to be "MundoSK-Name". This really only matters if anybody is going to actually have the player head in their inventory (i.e. if the player head is only going to be used as a block, the owner isn't important, though it may be useful as an identifier).

syntax_3394.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3394.title: Display Name of Player Tab
syntax_3394.description: An expression for the display name of the specified player tab in the specified or all tablist(s). This will not be set if the player tab's display name has not been changed (or was reset), or the player tab is hidden.
syntax_3394.syntax_pattern:
[the] [display] name of [the] [player] tab of %player% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
[the] [display] name of %player%'s [player] tab [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
[the] [tablisknu] tablist name of %player% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
%player%'s [tablisknu] tablist name [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]

en: An expression for the display name of the specified player tab in the specified or all tablist(s). This will not be set if the player tab's display name has not been changed (or was reset), or the player tab is hidden.

syntax_3395.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3395.title: Latency Bars of Player Tab
syntax_3395.description: An expression for the amount of latency bars of the specified player tab in the specified or all tablist(s). When set, this is always between 0 and 5. This will not be set if the player tab is hidden.
syntax_3395.syntax_pattern:
[the] (latency|ping) [bars] of [the] [player] tab of %player% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
(latency|ping) [bars] of %player%'s [player] tab [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
[the] tablist (latency|ping) [bars] of %player% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
%player%'s tablist (latency|ping) [bars] [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]

en: An expression for the amount of latency bars of the specified player tab in the specified or all tablist(s). When set, this is always between 0 and 5. This will not be set if the player tab is hidden.

syntax_3396.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3396.title: Score of Player Tab
syntax_3396.description: An expression for the score of the specified player tab in the specified or all tablist(s). This will not be set if the player tab is hidden.
syntax_3396.syntax_pattern:
[the] score of [the] [player] tab of %player% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
[the] score of %player%'s [player] tab [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
[the] tablist score of %player% [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]
%player%'s tablist score [for (%-players%|group %-string%)]

en: An expression for the score of the specified player tab in the specified or all tablist(s). This will not be set if the player tab is hidden.

syntax_3387.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3387.title: Display Name of Array Tab
syntax_3387.description: An expression for the display name of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s).

en: An expression for the display name of the specified array tab in the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3401.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3401.title: Score of Simple Tab
syntax_3401.description: An expression for the score of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s).

en: An expression for the score of the simple tab with the specified id in the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3402.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3402.title: Displayed Skin of Player
syntax_3402.description: An expression for the skin currently being displayed as the specified player's skin. If target ('for') players are specified, the expression will return a skin for each target player specified. Excluded players are meant to be specified only when setting the expression (for example, to prevent the original specified player from seeing a change). If the expression is evaluated with excluded players specified, it will act the same as if no target or excluded players had been specified.

en: An expression for the skin currently being displayed as the specified player's skin. If target ('for') players are specified, the expression will return a skin for each target player specified. Excluded players are meant to be specified only when setting the expression (for example, to prevent the original specified player from seeing a change). If the expression is evaluated with excluded players specified, it will act the same as if no target or excluded players had been specified.

syntax_3403.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3403.title: Nametag of Player
syntax_3403.description: An expression for the nametag (the name that appears above a player's head) of the specified player. If target ('for') players are specified, the expression will return a nametag for each target player specified.

en: An expression for the nametag (the name that appears above a player's head) of the specified player. If target ('for') players are specified, the expression will return a nametag for each target player specified.

syntax_3404.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3404.title: Skin of Player or Skull
syntax_3404.description: An expression for the skin of the specified player (must be online), player head item, or placed player head block.

en: An expression for the skin of the specified player (must be online), player head item, or placed player head block.

syntax_3405.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3405.title: Skin with Value
syntax_3405.description: An expression for a skin with the specified value and signature.

en: An expression for a skin with the specified value and signature.

syntax_3406.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3406.title: Default Tablist Icon
syntax_3406.description: This is the icon initally applied when creating tabs in an array tablist or when creating a simple tab without specifying the icon.

en: This is the icon initally applied when creating tabs in an array tablist or when creating a simple tab without specifying the icon.

syntax_3407.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3407.title: Height of Tablist Header or Footer
syntax_3407.description: The amount of lines in the header or footer of the specified or all tablist(s).
syntax_3407.syntax_pattern:
[the] height of [the] tablist (header|footer)[s] [(for|of) (%-players%|group %-string%)]
[the] height of [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist (header|footer)[s]

en: The amount of lines in the header or footer of the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3408.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3408.title: Line of Tablist Header or Footer
syntax_3408.description: A particular line of the header or footer of the specified or all tablist(s).
syntax_3408.syntax_pattern:
(line %-number%|[the] last line) of [the] tablist (header|footer)[s] [(for|of) (%-players%|group %-string%)]
(line %-number%|[the] last line) of [(%-players%|group %-string%)'[s]] tablist (header|footer)[s]

en: A particular line of the header or footer of the specified or all tablist(s).

syntax_3409.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3409.title: Tablist Header or Footer
syntax_3409.description: An expression for the header or footer of the specified or all tablist(s). This is a list expression as the header and footer can have multiple lines of text.

en: An expression for the header or footer of the specified or all tablist(s). This is a list expression as the header and footer can have multiple lines of text.

syntax_3410.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3410.title: Members of Tablist Group
syntax_3410.description: This is a list of the players who are currently in the specified tablist group.
syntax_3410.syntax_pattern:
[the] members of tablist group %string%
tablist group %string%'s members

en: This is a list of the players who are currently in the specified tablist group.

syntax_3411.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3411.title: Skin Texture
syntax_3411.description: Represents a skin, possibly of a player. Write 'steve' or 'alex' for these respective skins.

en: Represents a skin, possibly of a player. Write 'steve' or 'alex' for these respective skins.

syntax_3440.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3440.title: Retrieve Skin
syntax_3440.description: Retrieves a certain skin from online and places it into the specified variable (the object expression at the end). The retrieved skin is either a skin made from the specified image file, a skin from the specified URL, or the skin of either the specified offline player or the offline player with the specified uuid. If you are retrieving a skin made from an image file or from a URL, you can specify it as slim, meaning it would have the smaller arm size like the default Alex skin does, as opposed to the larger arms of the default Steve skin. Note that since this effect has to retrieve data from online APIs, it is a delayed effect and all code following the effect will also be delayed. Tablisknu uses the Mineskin API for retrieving skins based on image files and URLs.
syntax_3440.syntax_pattern:
retrieve [(slim)] skin from (file|url) %-string% [[with] timeout %-timespan%] into %object%
retrieve skin (from uuid %-string%|of %-offlineplayer%|from blueprint %-blueprint%) [[with] timeout %-timespan%] into %object%

en: Retrieves a certain skin from online and places it into the specified variable (the object expression at the end). The retrieved skin is either a skin made from the specified image file, a skin from the specified URL, or the skin of either the specified offline player or the offline player with the specified uuid. If you are retrieving a skin made from an image file or from a URL, you can specify it as slim, meaning it would have the smaller arm size like the default Alex skin does, as opposed to the larger arms of the default Steve skin. Note that since this effect has to retrieve data from online APIs, it is a delayed effect and all code following the effect will also be delayed. Tablisknu uses the Mineskin API for retrieving skins based on image files and URLs.

syntax_3383.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3383.title: Add Players to Tablist Group
syntax_3383.description: Adds the specified players to the specified tablist group. This means that you can make modifications to the tablist of the specified group, and those modifications will be applied to those players that have been added to the group. However, you can also modify the tablist of an individual player and the tablists of the rest of the group will be unchanged. Players can be added to multiple tablist groups without conflict as long as the modifications made to each tablist group are independent of each other, meaning they don't both, for example, change the display name of the same tab, or one of them change the display name while the other one hides the tab (you can see how that would conflict). The modifications that have been previously applied to the specified tablist group will be applied to the specified players (modifications that have become redundant will not be applied).

en: Adds the specified players to the specified tablist group. This means that you can make modifications to the tablist of the specified group, and those modifications will be applied to those players that have been added to the group. However, you can also modify the tablist of an individual player and the tablists of the rest of the group will be unchanged. Players can be added to multiple tablist groups without conflict as long as the modifications made to each tablist group are independent of each other, meaning they don't both, for example, change the display name of the same tab, or one of them change the display name while the other one hides the tab (you can see how that would conflict). The modifications that have been previously applied to the specified tablist group will be applied to the specified players (modifications that have become redundant will not be applied).

syntax_3441.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3441.title: All Full Color Skins
syntax_3441.description: A list expression for all the full color skins that are available using the Full Color Skin expression. Useful for giving your tabs a bit of color variety without having to specify specific colors.

en: A list expression for all the full color skins that are available using the Full Color Skin expression. Useful for giving your tabs a bit of color variety without having to specify specific colors.

syntax_3442.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3442.title: Full Color Skin
syntax_3442.description: An expression for a full color skin.
syntax_3442.syntax_pattern:
black skin
blue skin
brown skin
cyan skin
gray skin
green skin
light blue skin
light gray skin
lime skin
magenta skin
orange skin
pink skin
purple skin
red skin
white skin
yellow skin
maroon skin
navy skin
olive skin

en: An expression for a full color skin.
